provenance.source_corpus: EVALUATION OF SPAWNING SUBSTRATE ENHANCEMENT FOR WHITE STURGEON IN A REGULATED RIVER: EFFECTS ON LARVAL RETENTION AND DISPERSAL

source_spans.text: EVALUATION OF SPAWNING SUBSTRATE ENHANCEMENT FOR WHITE STURGEON IN A REGULATED RIVER
Results from this work indicated that the modified section of riverbed retained significantly higher numbers of larvae after release compared with the control site.

Results from this study demonstrate that modiﬁcations to embedded substrates at known white sturgeon spawning locations can result in enhanced conditions required for hiding at the yolk sac larvae stage.
Larvae at the modiﬁed site were able to hide and remain within the substrate and initiated downstream drift 15 days after release.
Larvae collected from the modiﬁed site were signiﬁcantly larger... compared with those collected at the control site
The regulation of rivers has altered the ecosystem processes important for the persistence of many aquatic species
The regulation of rivers typically results in an increase in substrate armouring and embeddedness.
As substrates become more armoured and embedded, the interstitial space between substrate particles is reduced
Decreasing interstitial space limits the available area and cover for fish eggs and larvae of multiple species, affecting embryo survival
Decreasing interstitial space limits the available area and cover for fish eggs and larvae of multiple species, affecting embryo survival, larval emergence and physical size
